column1: # reviewer-routing.tsv — single source of truth for the EXTENSION/PATH-GLOB
# axis of /build's 3e pre-push reviewer routing (ADR 0008,
# docs/adr/0008-reviewer-routing-tsv-extension-axis-scope.md).
# Scope (ADR 0008 Decision): this tsv owns every file-extension/path-glob
# route, INCLUDING the docs/** -> docs-reviewer glob. It carries NO
# per-checkout activation state — activation lives solely as
# symlink/copy presence in the gitignored .claude/agents/ (ADR 0007).
# The non-extension routing logic stays prose-resident in
# claude/commands/build.md 3e: the claude/commands/*.md -> workflow-reviewer
# override (temperloop#1007, which wins over any tsv row for that path),
# the `architectural` change-kind route (no file extension to key on), the
# per-item `review:` override, and the run-both multi-match rule.
# Format: TAB-separated, 3 columns, no header line consumed by tooling
# (this comment block documents them):
#   1. extension-or-path-glob   e.g. `.py` or `docs/**`
#   2. reviewer-name            the review subagent's declared name
#   3. catalog-agent-path       repo-relative path to its agent definition
# Invariant: no extension/glob is claimed by two rows (checked by
# check-reviewer-routing.sh, wired into scripts/quality-gates.sh).
# Two rows may name the SAME reviewer for different extensions (.ts and .js
# both -> typescript-reviewer) — that is not a duplicate claim.
# The seven-language roster (ADR 0007) ships as an INERT kernel catalog —
# claude/agents/reviewers/<name>.md is the planned catalog location; an
# un-activated reviewer is never deployed into a live .claude/agents/ and
# costs nothing until a repo opts in.
.py
.sh
.ts
.js
.go
.rs
.java
.swift
docs/**
